message.text: S3 bucket site: versioning is not enabled. Accidental or malicious deletes are not recoverable.
S3 bucket policy on alb_logs does not reference aws:SecureTransport — traffic can potentially be accepted over plain HTTP.
Lambda function api: code_signing_config_arn is not set. Any artifact pushed to the function passes — there's no signing-publisher gate.
Lambda function api: no explicit aws_cloudwatch_log_group declared. Lambda will auto-create one with default retention (never expires) and AES256 encryption — no CMK, no controlled retention.
KMS key eks: deletion_window_in_days is less than the maximum of 30. If scheduled for deletion, there is less time to cancel and recover data encrypted with this key.
KMS key logs: deletion_window_in_days is less than the maximum of 30. If scheduled for deletion, there is less time to cancel and recover data encrypted with this key.
Secrets Manager secret db_password: verify a rotation schedule is configured (aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation with rotation_rules.automatically_after_days <= 90).
ECS cluster main: Container Insights is not enabled. CPU/memory/network metrics aren't aggregated; capacity-planning and incident triage rely on raw task-level metrics.
EKS cluster this: control-plane logging does not include `audit`. Forensic investigation of cluster-level access (kubectl, CRD operations, RBAC misuse) is impossible after the fact.
CloudFront distribution site: no WAF web ACL is associated. L7 attacks (SQLi, XSS, scrapers, credential stuffing) hit the origin directly.
CloudFront distribution site: access logging is not configured. Forensic trace of viewer requests is unavailable; cache-hit-rate analysis and abuse investigation are blocked.

Subnet public: map_public_ip_on_launch = true. Every instance launched into this subnet receives a public IP automatically — including future instances created by ASGs, ECS, or EKS without an explicit private-only override.

Subnet public: map_public_ip_on_launch = true. Every instance launched into this subnet receives a public IP automatically — including future instances created by ASGs, ECS, or EKS without an explicit private-only override.

Subnet public: map_public_ip_on_launch = true. Every instance launched into this subnet receives a public IP automatically — including future instances created by ASGs, ECS, or EKS without an explicit private-only override.

Lambda api: no aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm references this function. Errors land in CloudWatch but nobody is paged. The next time this Lambda starts throwing 500s, the on-call learns about it from a customer ticket.

Lambda api: no alarm on Throttles. When this function gets throttled (own cap or account cap), invocations fail silently from the caller's perspective — neither logs nor metrics-as-configured surface it as a paging signal.
ALB main: no alarm references this load balancer. Target health is a load-balancer concern — without a paging hook, a target group running with 50% healthy hosts is silent until the last host fails.

RDS main: no alarms reference this instance. CPU saturation and connection-pool exhaustion are the two failure modes engineers don't catch until customers complain. Both have CloudWatch metrics; neither has a configured threshold here.

SQS queue lambda_dlq: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

Lambda api: this plan declares no aws_cloudwatch_dashboard. If incidents are debugged by typing CloudWatch URLs into the AWS console one tab at a time, the next on-call engineer will spend their first 5 minutes rebuilding mental state someone else already had.
ALB main: internet-facing, no aws_route53_health_check references it. DNS keeps pointing at this LB even when it's serving 5xx — no upstream failover.

S3 bucket versioning resource site: versioning_configuration.status = local.cfg.versioning_enabled ? "Enabled" : "Disabled". An overwrite or DeleteObject is permanent — no undo, no audit of what the prior content was.
IAM policy ecs_execution_secrets: grants a high-privilege action with no Condition block. Compromise of any principal holding this policy gives unconstrained use of that action.
EIP nat: no instance, network_interface, or associate_with_private_ip set. If nothing else binds this in another file, AWS charges for an unused static IP every month.

logs: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.

Plan includes hourly-billed resources (1 aws_db_instance, 2× aws_instance, 7× aws_eks_cluster, 8× aws_nat_gateway) but no aws_budgets_budget. Estimated monthly spend from priceable resources in this plan: ~$263/month (us-east-1 on-demand). Without a cost alarm, a forgotten instance, misconfigured autoscaling, or credential compromise can run up an unbounded bill before the team notices.

S3 buckets (assets, site) have no aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration. Without lifecycle rules, objects accumulate forever: old versions from versioning, abandoned multipart uploads, and stale data all bill at $0.023/GB-month with no bound.

Plan includes aws_nat_gateway.this and 4 S3 buckets (assets, alb_logs, logs, site) but no aws_vpc_endpoint for S3. All S3 API calls from private subnets flow through the NAT gateway at $0.045/GB data-processing charge. An S3 Gateway endpoint is completely free and eliminates that cost entirely.
A Lambda function with publish = true is being referenced via its unversioned arn. For Lambda@Edge associations, this means edits replicate to all edges before validation against canary traffic.

SQS queue lambda_dlq: no redrive_policy (no dead-letter queue) and no kms_master_key_id. Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)
IAM role cluster: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.

fullDescription.text: VPC this: no aws_vpc_endpoint for KMS. Decrypt calls leave the VPC via NAT — for high-volume workloads this adds up faster than the endpoint cost.

shortDescription.text: VPC has no KMS VPC endpoint

fullDescription.text: Cluster this: no aws_vpc_endpoint declared for ECR or S3. Container image pulls + Docker layer downloads flow through NAT — common single-biggest contributor to NAT data-processing charges.

shortDescription.text: EKS / ECS cluster present but no ECR VPC endpoints

fullDescription.text: EKS cluster this: enabled_cluster_log_types missing `audit`. Cluster-level API access is invisible — no record of who called what during an incident or audit.

shortDescription.text: EKS cluster control plane logging types incomplete

fullDescription.text: this: no aws_inspector2_enabler in the plan. Container / runtime / EC2 CVEs aren't surfaced — the team is doing vulnerability management manually or not at all.

shortDescription.text: No aws_inspector2_enabler declared (no vulnerability scanning)

fullDescription.text: S3 bucket site: versioning is not enabled. Accidental or malicious deletes are not recoverable.

shortDescription.text: S3 bucket should have versioning enabled

fullDescription.text: S3 bucket policy on alb_logs does not reference aws:SecureTransport — traffic can potentially be accepted over plain HTTP.

shortDescription.text: S3 bucket policy should deny non-TLS (HTTP) access

fullDescription.text: Secrets Manager secret db_password: verify a rotation schedule is configured (aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation with rotation_rules.automatically_after_days <= 90).

shortDescription.text: Secrets Manager secret should have automatic rotation configured

fullDescription.text: SQS queue lambda_dlq: no redrive_policy (no dead-letter queue) and no kms_master_key_id. Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)

shortDescription.text: SQS queue has no dead-letter queue and no customer-managed KMS key

fullDescription.text: Security group permits 0.0.0.0/0 on a known debug or management port: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: ingress.cidr_blocks

shortDescription.text: [Needs Review] Security group permits 0.0.0.0/0 on a known debug or management port

fullDescription.text: Subnet public: map_public_ip_on_launch = true. Every instance launched into this subnet receives a public IP automatically — including future instances created by ASGs, ECS, or EKS without an explicit private-only override.

shortDescription.text: VPC subnet automatically assigns public IPs on launch

fullDescription.text: A Lambda function with publish = true is being referenced via its unversioned arn. For Lambda@Edge associations, this means edits replicate to all edges before validation against canary traffic.

shortDescription.text: Lambda@Edge candidate api is referenced via .arn (not .qualified_arn) — no version lock
